LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT DON WARD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FRAUD, MISREPRESENTATION AND ELDER ABUSE. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND; NO OPPOSITION FILED AS TO CONSTRUCTIVE FRAUD AND CONSTRUCTIVE TRUST. PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD FACTS DESCRIBING HOW IT IS BELL WAS GIVEN PLAINTIFF'S POWER OF ATTORNEY IN 2003; PLAINTIFF TO ATTACH OR DESCRIBE THE TERMS OF BELL'S POWER OF ATTORNEY; PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD FACTS RELATED TO THE TRANSFER OF THE PROPERTY TO ALL SAINTS AND PLAINTIFF'S KNOWLEDGE, IF ANY, OF THE TRANSFER; PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD THE FAIR MARKET VALUE OF THE PROPERTY AND THE AMOUNT PAID BY WARD FOR THE PROPERTY; AND PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD, WITH GREATER PRECISION, WHEN BIVENS LEARNED THE PROPERTY WAS FOR "SALE". Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Jul-13-2010 Continued To Jul-27-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 218
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr., Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

John F. Vannucci, Esq. appeared with plaintif Emma Quijada, who was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Juan Romero.
Default was entered against defendant Juan Romero on July 14, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed on July 1, 2010.
Dismissal with prejudice as to defendant Sonia Cassia was filed on December 17, 2008.
Plaintiff's Prove-Up Brief was filed today, July 13, 2010.
No Proof of Service of the Statement of Damages in the case file.

The Court requires further information from plaintiff in order to proceed with today's default prove-up hearing. Good cause appearing, this matter is CONTINUED to July 20, 2010 @ 9:00 a.m. in Department 218. Plaintiff's appearance is waived at the next calling of the case.

Counsel shall submit a proposed judgment to the Court for review no later than July 14, 2010.

Off Calendar Dec-17-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DON WARD'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: J. Scott Weaver, Esq. of Wartelle, Weaver & Schreiber 693-0504 for pltf & opposing party; Gwen L. Fanger, Esq. of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP 276-6567 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. THE AMENDED COMPLAINT ADDRESSES THE ISSUES THAT WERE THE SUBJECT OF THE PRIOR DEMURRER. Order signed in open court. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-14-2008 Continued To Feb-02-09 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-04-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT DON WARD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to causes of action for fraud, misrepresentation and elder abuse. Sustained without leave to amend; no opposition filed as to constructive fraud and constructive trust. Plaintiff to plead facts describing how it is Bell was given plaintiff's power of attorney in 2003; plaintiff to attach or describe the terms of Bell's power of attorney; plaintiff to plead facts related to the transfer of the property to All Saints and plaintiff's knowledge, if any, of the transfer; plaintiff to plead the fair market value of the property and the amount paid by Ward for the property; and plaintiff to plead, with greater precision, when Bivens learned the property was for "sale". Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
